projectDescription: A generic web-based inspector and client for Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, built with React + Vite, that lets you connect to any MCP server, explore its tools, prompts, and resources, and execute them interactively from the browser.
keyFeatures: Connect to any MCP server via Streamable HTTP or SSE transport
Auto-discover tools, prompts, and resources after MCP handshake
Dynamic form generation from JSON Schema (inputSchema) for tools and prompts
Raw JSON argument mode for advanced usage
Real-time activity console logging all requests and responses
Docker multi-stage build for containerized deployment via nginx
publicApiSurface: McpConnection.connect(config) — establishes MCP connection, returns serverInfo, capabilities, tools, prompts, resources
McpConnection.callTool(name, args) — invokes a tool on the connected server
McpConnection.getPrompt(name, args) — retrieves a prompt from the connected server
McpConnection.disconnect() — closes the active MCP connection
McpConnection.connected — boolean getter indicating connection state
Dropdown component — reusable accessible dropdown (value, options, onChange, disabled, placeholder)
ConsoleLog component — activity log display (entries, onClear)
Transport options: http (Streamable HTTP), sse (Server-Sent Events)
